records.title: What They Remember of Me
records.about: A digital album built from early memories told through the voices of my family, capturing moments from 2000 to 2010 that helped shape the foundation of who I am.
records.description: What They Remember of Me is a memory-driven collection created from stories shared by my grandmothers, my mom, and my Aunt Bobbie. Each track reflects a moment, perspective, or piece of my early life as seen through the people who were there. Instead of a single narrative, the album is built from different viewpoints, blending together to form a picture of my beginnings. It captures small moments, meaningful memories, and the impressions I left before I was old enough to fully understand them myself.

records.title: Too Far Gone
records.about: a song about the monster inside taking over.
records.description: A track centered on losing control to something darker inside. It reflects a period where inner struggle stopped feeling distant and started taking over in a way that felt real, personal, and dangerous.

records.title: Beaten Down
records.about: Enduring years of bullying while refusing to break.
records.description: This track reflects a prolonged period of pressure and repeated hits, where the weight never really lets up. It carries the feeling of being pushed down, worn thin, and forced to hold ground with little relief. At its core, it’s about not giving in finding a way to stand back up, keep a voice, and turn that pressure into something that doesn’t break you.

records.title: Anything But Me
records.about: Hiding who I am to avoid being targeted
records.description: This track reflects a period where being myself didn’t feel like an option. It’s about adapting to every room, reading people, and becoming whatever kept attention off me. Instead of pushing back, I learned to disappear—blending in, shutting down, and cutting off parts of myself before anyone else could. It’s survival through control, where staying unseen felt safer than being known

records.title: Same Day Again
records.about: Stuck in the same cycle, day after day.
records.description: This track sits between losing myself and looking back on it. It reflects a stretch where everything blurred together—nothing changing, nothing improving, just repeating the same patterns over and over. Not as intense as what came before, but not resolved either, it carries the feeling of being stuck in motion without actually moving forward

records.title: Under the Tree
records.about: A song tied to my dad's death.
records.description: One of the most directly personal tracks on the album, this song centers on the loss of my dad. It marks a defining moment in my life and carries the weight, memory, and lasting emotional impact of that event.

records.title: Love Is Dead
records.about: Losing love and everything tied to it.
records.description: This track reflects a point where love didn’t just end, it collapsed completely, taking control, identity, and stability with it. It captures the feeling of giving everything to something that ultimately breaks, leaving nothing left to hold onto. In that aftermath, there’s both emptiness and a strange sense of release, where what was lost also removes what once held me in place.

records.title: Who I Was
records.about: Realizing I was the one holding myself back.
records.description: This track marks a shift in perspective, where everything I believed about what I was reacting to starts to change. It reflects the realization that the control, distance, and self-restriction I lived in weren’t just reactions to others, they were choices I continued to make. What once felt like defense starts to look like self-imposed limits, and the line between protection and confinement becomes clear. It’s not a breakdown or a breakthrough, just a quiet understanding that the barrier was never fully external.

records.title: Free To Be Me
records.about: Stepping into who I am without apology.
records.description: This track closes on identity, freedom, and self-acceptance. After everything before it, this song represents reaching a place where I can stand as myself more fully, without hiding, bending, or being defined only by what happened to me.

records.title: Grounded Fire
records.about: A song built from affirmations focused on confidence and boldness.
records.description: Grounded Fire is designed to reinforce both stability and courage. The song emphasizes remaining grounded while still having the confidence to move forward and take decisive action.

records.title: The Perfect One For Me
records.about: A reflection on the qualities I hope to find in a romantic partner.
records.description: This song was created from a personal list describing the kind of partner I would like to build a life with. While it describes an ideal, the song also acknowledges that real people will never perfectly match a list. One lyric intentionally adds humor to the idea: “She can cook, she can clean, and she can playfully get me to do it for her.”

records.title: More Than Enough Time
records.about: A song built from affirmations centered on time and perspective.
records.description: Many people feel constant pressure from the belief that there is never enough time. This song reframes that perspective by encouraging a calmer mindset where time is abundant and manageable.

records.title: Already In Changing Motion
records.about: A reflection on a year filled with overlapping life transitions.
records.description: This song was written about a period where multiple major life changes were happening at once. School, work, volunteering, business projects, and searching for a home all began moving forward simultaneously, creating the feeling of constant momentum.

records.title: Easy Semester
records.about: A mindset track meant to reinforce a calm and manageable semester.
records.description: As a full-time student, some semesters become overwhelming. This song was written to reinforce a mindset of balance and confidence as I approach the final stretch of my academic program.

records.title: Aligned With You
records.about: A song about aligning energy with the right romantic partner.
records.description: Instead of focusing on finding a specific person, this song centers on becoming aligned with the type of relationship that naturally fits. It reflects the belief that the right partnership comes from compatibility in mindset and direction.

records.title: Inevitable Wealth
records.about: A song focused on opening one's mindset to financial opportunity.
records.description: Written during my final year of school, this track focuses on shifting perspective from financial stress toward opportunity and possibility.

records.title: The Right Home
records.about: A mindset piece about finding and purchasing the right home.
records.description: Searching for a home is both practical and emotional. This song focuses on reinforcing the mindset that the right place will appear at the right time.

records.title: What Time Stole Away
records.about: A transitional piece connecting the search in "Decade Search" to the events of "Second Meeting."
records.description: This song acts as a bridge within the album's storyline, reflecting on how time changes memories and expectations. It captures the how time can make memories blurry or just fade away.

records.title: Decade Search
records.about: A narrative song based on journal entries documenting a long search for a childhood friend.
records.description: In 2015 I began searching for a childhood friend I had lost contact with. Over the years I wrote journal entries documenting the search and the thoughts that came with it. This song adapts those entries into a condensed narrative, shaping years of reflection into a single story-driven track.

records.title: Second Meeting
records.about: A song describing a real encounter that followed reconnecting with someone.
records.description: After reconnecting with a friend through conversation, we eventually met for coffee. This song captures the mix of familiarity and uncertainty that can come from meeting someone again after time apart. This is the second version of the song. The revision involved a small lyrical change to better match how the moment was remembered — how the same eyes could appear different depending on the light.

records.title: The Unreachable Flower
records.about: A reflection on longing for something meaningful that may always remain out of reach.
records.description: This song explores the emotional tension between hope and acceptance. It focuses on pursuing something beautiful or meaningful while recognizing that it may never fully be attained.

records.title: Fortress Of My Mind
records.about: A song about emotional defenses built from past experiences.
records.description: After being hurt in the past, I built emotional walls to avoid repeating those experiences. This song tells the story of someone who managed to break through those defenses for a short time before the relationship came to an end.

records.title: I Can't Get You Out Of My Head
records.about: A song about the persistence of attraction and lingering thoughts.
records.description: This track describes the experience of having someone constantly on your mind no matter how much you try to focus on other things. The repetition of those thoughts becomes both frustrating and strangely compelling.

records.title: Two Months And A Message
records.about: A reflection on communicating with someone who replies on their own timeline.
records.description: This song was inspired by a friend who sometimes takes a long time to respond to messages. However, when they do reply, their responses are thoughtful and detailed. The song balances the frustration of waiting with appreciation for the depth of those conversations.

records.title: Knight In Shining Armor
records.about: A rewritten version of one of the earliest songs I wrote.
records.description: This song was originally written when I was younger and far more idealistic. Revisiting it years later felt both nostalgic and slightly embarrassing, but instead of discarding it I rewrote and expanded the song while preserving the original core theme.

records.title: Wild And Free
records.about: A reflection on a restless and constantly wandering mind.
records.description: This song was written during a time when focusing on a single idea felt nearly impossible. My thoughts constantly jumped between new plans and possibilities until I eventually learned to embrace that creative wandering instead of resisting it.

records.title: The Quiet Vow
records.about: A song written in the style of traditional sword dance music.
records.description: I enjoy historical and medieval dances, especially sword dances. Because there are relatively few modern songs designed for them, I wrote this piece to fill that gap while still fitting within the narrative of the album.

records.title: The Parts You Don't See
records.about: A personal reflection on the deeper aspects of identity.
records.description: People often ask how to describe yourself, but most introductions only capture surface details. This song explores the deeper motivations, experiences, and perspectives that shape who I really am.

records.title: Delightfully Late Responder
records.about: A lighthearted reflection on a friend who takes a long time to reply to messages.
records.description: This song was written about a friend who often takes weeks or months to respond to messages. Despite the delays, their replies are thoughtful and detailed, often arriving as long multi-paragraph responses. Rather than seeing them as a bad texter, I prefer to think of them as a delightfully late responder.
